zh.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II於1470年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾凱特林。他和Mary Anne Poyser至少有1個兒子。他去世於1527年在他的家鄉享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dalyson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: patronymic or metronymic meaning ‘son of the dairyman or dairywoman’ (from Middle English day(e), dey(e) + son).
variant of Dawson with the original diphthong /au/ simplified to /a:/ in late Middle English and subsequently pronounced /εɪ/. Compare Dakin for Dawkin (see Dawkins ).

variants.name: Dawson
meanings: English: patronymic meaning ‘son of Dawe’, from the Middle English personal name Daw(e) (see Daw 1 and 2), which is sometimes a pet form of David (see 2 below) but more generally of Rauf or Raw (i.e. Ralph ), of which Daw(e) is a rhyming form. Alternatively, the patronymic could mean ‘son of Daud(e)’, another Middle English pet form of Ralph evidently used interchangeably with Dawe. Dawson is one of the most numerous patronymics.
Scottish: patronymic, ‘son of Dawe’. In Scotland Dawe is a diminutive of David (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Deason
meanings: English: variant of   Dayson   in either sense.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Norwegian Disen (see Dyson 2).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Johnathon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: respelled form of   Jonathan  , influenced by   John .

variants.name: Jonathan
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘God has given’, composed of the same elements as those of   Matthew  , but in reverse order. This is the name of several characters in the Bible, most notably a son of King Saul, who was a devoted friend and supporter of the young David, even when David and Saul were themselves at loggerheads (1 Samuel 31; 2 Samuel 1:19–26). The name is often taken as symbolic of steadfast friendship and loyalty. Well-known bearers of the name include the Irish clergyman and writer  Jonathan Swift  (  1667-1745 ), British theatre director  Jonathan Miller  (  b. 1934 ), British actor  Jonathan Pryce  (  b. 1947 ), and British television presenter  Jonathan Ross  (  b. 1960 ).

en.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II was born in 1470, in Kettering, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Mary Anne Poyser. He died in 1527, in his hometown, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II est né en 1470, à Kettering, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mary Anne Poyser. Il est décédé en 1527, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II wurde 1470 in Kettering, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Mary Anne Poyser mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1527 im Alter von 57 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II è nato il nel 1470 nel Kettering, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mary Anne Poyser. Morì nel 1527, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, IIは1470年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアケッテリングで生まれた。彼はMary Anne Poyserとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1527年に故郷で57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II(은)는 1470년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 케테링에서 태어났다. 그는 Mary Anne Poyser(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1527년 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II nasceu em 1470, em Kettering, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mary Anne Poyser. Ele faleceu em 1527, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II родился в 1470 г. в местности кеттиринг, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mary Anne Poyser. Он умер в 1527 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Johnathon de Dalyson of Cranesley Lord, II nació en 1470, en Kettering, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mary Anne Poyser. Murió en 1527, en su pueblo, a la edad de 57 años.
